skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to replace a large fixed upfront server purchase with an expense you pay only as you consume capacity. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: Pay-as-you-go pricing is correct because Pay-as-you-go converts fixed capital expense into variable expense billed only for what you use. Distractors: Economies of scale lowers prices as aggregated usage grows. Elasticity scales capacity up and down to match demand. Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions quickly.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: A design review identifies this requirement: get progressively lower prices because AWS aggregates demand from hundreds of thousands of customers. What should you configure?
explanation: Economies of scale is correct because Aggregated usage gives AWS economies of scale that translate into lower pay-as-you-go prices over time. Distractors: Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions quickly. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compares full on-premises versus cloud cost. Elasticity scales capacity up and down to match demand.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to automatically add and remove capacity so you reduce paying for idle servers and avoid running short at peak?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Elasticity is correct because Elasticity acquires resources when needed and releases them when not, reducing over-provisioning. Distractors: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting removes data-center operational work. Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions quickly. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compares full on-premises versus cloud cost.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: launch the same application in data centers on other continents within minutes; and provision a new test environment in minutes instead of waiting weeks for hardware.
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Go global in minutes is correct: AWS global infrastructure lets you deploy to multiple Regions worldwide quickly for lower latency to users. Increased speed and agility is correct: In the cloud new resources are a click away, cutting provisioning from weeks to minutes. Elasticity scales capacity up and down to match demand. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compares full on-premises versus cloud cost. Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting removes data-center operational work.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: An operations team wants to compare the relevant direct and indirect costs of running on premises versus on AWS. Which choice is most appropriate?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) is correct because TCO compares relevant direct and indirect costs; AWS avoids physical data-center work, though customers still administer their workloads. Distractors: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting removes data-center operational work. Increased speed and agility makes new resources available in minutes. Stop guessing capacity removes the need to forecast capacity.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: During implementation, you must let engineers stop racking, powering, and cooling servers so they focus on the product. Which AWS feature should you choose?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting is correct because AWS removes the undifferentiated work of running data centers so teams focus on what differentiates the business. Distractors: Stop guessing capacity removes the need to forecast capacity. Pay-as-you-go pricing bills only for what you consume. Increased speed and agility makes new resources available in minutes.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: An organization has the following requirement: provision a new test environment in minutes instead of waiting weeks for hardware. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: Increased speed and agility is correct because In the cloud new resources are a click away, cutting provisioning from weeks to minutes. Distractors: Economies of scale lowers prices as aggregated usage grows. Stop guessing capacity removes the need to forecast capacity. Pay-as-you-go pricing bills only for what you consume.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: avoid buying too much or too little hardware by no longer forecasting capacity before launch; and automatically add and remove capacity so you reduce paying for idle servers and avoid running short at peak?
explanation: Stop guessing capacity is correct: You scale on demand rather than guessing capacity in advance, avoiding waste or shortfalls. Elasticity is correct: Elasticity acquires resources when needed and releases them when not, reducing over-provisioning. Pay-as-you-go pricing bills only for what you consume. Economies of scale lowers prices as aggregated usage grows. Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions quickly.

skill: Identify the pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework
question: What should be configured first when the goal is to run and monitor systems to deliver business value and continuously improve supporting processes?
explanation: Operational Excellence pillar is correct because The Operational Excellence pillar focuses on running, monitoring, and continuously improving systems and processes. Distractors: Security pillar is the pillar for protecting systems and data. Reliability pillar is the pillar for recovering from failure and meeting demand. Performance Efficiency pillar is the pillar for using resources efficiently.

skill: Identify the pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework
question: Your team needs to protect information and systems through identity, detection, and data protection. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Security pillar is correct because The Security pillar covers identity and access, detection, infrastructure and data protection, and incident response. Distractors: Performance Efficiency pillar is the pillar for using resources efficiently. Cost Optimization pillar is the pillar for avoiding unnecessary cost. Reliability pillar is the pillar for recovering from failure and meeting demand.

skill: Identify the pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework
question: To ensure a workload performs its intended function correctly and recovers from failures, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Reliability pillar is correct because The Reliability pillar focuses on recovering from failures and consistently meeting demand. Distractors: Sustainability pillar is the pillar for minimizing environmental impact. Performance Efficiency pillar is the pillar for using resources efficiently. Cost Optimization pillar is the pillar for avoiding unnecessary cost.

skill: Identify the pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework
question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: use computing resources efficiently and maintain that efficiency as demand changes; and run and monitor systems to deliver business value and continuously improve supporting processes.
explanation: Performance Efficiency pillar is correct: The Performance Efficiency pillar focuses on using resources efficiently as requirements evolve. Operational Excellence pillar is correct: The Operational Excellence pillar focuses on running, monitoring, and continuously improving systems and processes. Security pillar is the pillar for protecting systems and data. Reliability pillar is the pillar for recovering from failure and meeting demand. Cost Optimization pillar is the pillar for avoiding unnecessary cost.

skill: Identify the pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework
question: A workload requires that you avoid unnecessary cost and understand where money is being spent. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Cost Optimization pillar is correct because The Cost Optimization pillar focuses on avoiding unneeded cost and getting the best value for spend. Distractors: Sustainability pillar is the pillar for minimizing environmental impact. Operational Excellence pillar is the pillar for running and improving systems. Security pillar is the pillar for protecting systems and data.

skill: Identify the pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework
question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to minimize the environmental impact and energy use of running cloud workloads. What do you select?
explanation: Sustainability pillar is correct because The Sustainability pillar focuses on reducing energy use and improving efficiency across the workload. Distractors: Security pillar is the pillar for protecting systems and data. Reliability pillar is the pillar for recovering from failure and meeting demand. Operational Excellence pillar is the pillar for running and improving systems.

skill: Identify AWS cloud design principles
question: Which AWS feature lets you assume any component can fail and add redundancy so the system keeps running?
options: Design for failure and build redundancy
explanation: Design for failure and build redundancy is correct because Designing for failure and adding redundancy removes single points of failure so the system tolerates component loss. Distractors: Use managed services shifts operational work to AWS. Loose coupling is a design principle reducing dependencies between components. Automation is a design principle replacing manual steps with code.

skill: Identify AWS cloud design principles
question: Which option should you implement to reduce dependencies between components so they can change or fail independently?
explanation: Loose coupling is correct because Loose coupling reduces interdependencies through defined interfaces so components evolve and fail independently. Distractors: Automation is a design principle replacing manual steps with code. Use managed services shifts operational work to AWS. Make data-driven decisions is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify AWS cloud design principles
question: You need a solution to replace manual, error-prone setup with repeatable code that applies changes consistently. Which option fits best?
options: Design for failure and build redundancy
explanation: Automation is correct because Automating with code reduces human error and makes changes consistent and repeatable. Distractors: Use managed services shifts operational work to AWS. Make data-driven decisions is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Design for failure and build redundancy is a design principle for removing single points of failure.

skill: Identify AWS cloud design principles
question: Two requirements must be met: offload undifferentiated operational work by letting AWS run the service infrastructure; and reduce dependencies between components so they can change or fail independently. Select the two correct options.
options: Design for failure and build redundancy
explanation: Use managed services is correct: Using AWS managed services shifts operational and maintenance work to AWS so teams focus on the application. Loose coupling is correct: Loose coupling reduces interdependencies through defined interfaces so components evolve and fail independently. Make data-driven decisions is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Design for failure and build redundancy is a design principle for removing single points of failure. Automation is a design principle replacing manual steps with code.

skill: Identify AWS cloud design principles
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to collect data about how the architecture performs and use it to choose and improve the design. Which option best meets the requirement?
options: Design for failure and build redundancy
explanation: Make data-driven decisions is correct because AWS recommends driving architecture decisions using data collected about how the workload actually performs. Distractors: Automation is a design principle replacing manual steps with code. Design for failure and build redundancy is a design principle for removing single points of failure. Loose coupling is a design principle reducing dependencies between components.

skill: Identify cloud deployment and service models
question: A design review identifies this requirement: run every part of an application in the cloud with no on-premises infrastructure. What should you configure?
explanation: Cloud (all-in) deployment model is correct because An all-in cloud deployment runs the entire application in the cloud. Distractors: Hybrid deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. On-premises (private cloud) deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify cloud deployment and service models
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to connect on-premises infrastructure with cloud resources so they work together?
explanation: Hybrid deployment model is correct because A hybrid deployment connects on-premises infrastructure with cloud resources, for example using AWS Outposts or VMware Cloud on AWS. Distractors: On-premises (private cloud) deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Platform as a Service (PaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify cloud deployment and service models
question: An operations team wants to keep running applications in your own data center using virtualization and resource management. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: On-premises (private cloud) deployment model is correct because An on-premises or private-cloud deployment runs applications in your own data center using virtualization and management tools. Distractors: Platform as a Service (PaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Software as a Service (SaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify cloud deployment and service models
question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: rent virtual servers, storage, and networking while you manage the operating system and applications; and run every part of an application in the cloud with no on-premises infrastructure.
explanation: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) is correct: IaaS provides the basic building blocks of compute, storage, and networking while the customer manages the OS and applications. Cloud (all-in) deployment model is correct: An all-in cloud deployment runs the entire application in the cloud. Hybrid deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. On-premises (private cloud) deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Platform as a Service (PaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify cloud deployment and service models
question: During implementation, you must use a managed platform to build and run applications without managing the underlying OS or servers. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: Platform as a Service (PaaS) is correct because PaaS removes the need to manage the underlying infrastructure and OS so you focus on deploying and running applications. Distractors: Software as a Service (SaaS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Cloud (all-in) deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Hybrid deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify cloud deployment and service models
question: An organization has the following requirement: use a complete application delivered over the internet without managing any infrastructure. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: Software as a Service (SaaS) is correct because SaaS delivers a complete, provider-managed application over the internet; the customer just uses it. Distractors: Cloud (all-in) deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Hybrid deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. On-premises (private cloud) deployment model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Understand cloud adoption and migration
question: What should be configured first when the goal is to organize a cloud adoption program across business, people, governance, platform, security, and operations?
explanation: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) is correct because The AWS CAF organizes adoption guidance into six perspectives to plan and run cloud adoption. Distractors: Replatform makes small cloud optimizations during migration. Repurchase switches to a different product such as SaaS. Rehost lifts and shifts with minimal change.

skill: Understand cloud adoption and migration
question: Your team needs to move an application to AWS with minimal changes by lifting and shifting it. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Rehost is correct because Rehosting (lift and shift) moves an application without changing its architecture, the fastest strategy. Distractors: Refactor re-architects the application to be cloud-native. Replatform makes small cloud optimizations during migration. Repurchase switches to a different product such as SaaS.

skill: Understand cloud adoption and migration
question: To move to a managed database during migration with a few optimizations but no rewrite, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Replatform is correct because Replatforming makes small cloud optimizations such as a managed database without re-architecting. Distractors: Repurchase switches to a different product such as SaaS. Refactor re-architects the application to be cloud-native. Retire decommissions an unneeded application.

skill: Understand cloud adoption and migration
question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: retire the existing product and switch to a different one, often SaaS; and keep some applications on premises for now and revisit migrating them later.
explanation: Repurchase is correct: Repurchasing replaces the current product with a different one, frequently a SaaS offering. Retain is correct: Retaining keeps applications where they are when there is no current business case to migrate. AWS Application Migration Service automates rehosting of servers. AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) organizes adoption across perspectives, not a single migration. Rehost lifts and shifts with minimal change.

skill: Understand cloud adoption and migration
question: A workload requires that you rework an application to be cloud-native, for example breaking it into microservices. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Refactor is correct because Refactoring significantly changes the application to take advantage of cloud-native features. Distractors: Retain keeps an application where it is for now. Relocate moves infrastructure without changing the application. Retire decommissions an unneeded application.

skill: Understand cloud adoption and migration
question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to decommission applications that are no longer useful instead of migrating them. What do you select?
explanation: Retire is correct because Retiring shuts down applications that are no longer needed, reducing cost and attack surface. Distractors: AWS DataSync moves large datasets to AWS online. Retain keeps an application where it is for now. Relocate moves infrastructure without changing the application.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which AWS feature lets you decide who installs operating system patches on an Amazon EC2 instance?
options: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
AWS provides physical data center security
Customer configures IAM users and permissions
explanation: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is correct because For EC2 the customer manages the guest operating system and its patches; AWS manages the host and hypervisor. Distractors: AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is an AWS infrastructure responsibility. AWS provides physical data center security is an AWS responsibility. Customer configures IAM users and permissions is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which option should you implement to decide who maintains the host hypervisor and the physical servers?
options: AWS provides physical data center security
Customer configures IAM users and permissions
Customer classifies and encrypts its data
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
explanation: AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is correct because AWS is responsible for the infrastructure that runs services, including the hypervisor and physical hardware. Distractors: AWS provides physical data center security is an AWS responsibility. Customer configures IAM users and permissions is a customer responsibility. Customer classifies and encrypts its data is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: You need a solution to decide who is responsible for the physical security of AWS data centers. Which option fits best?
options: Customer classifies and encrypts its data
AWS provides physical data center security
Customer configures IAM users and permissions
explanation: AWS provides physical data center security is correct because Physical and environmental security of AWS facilities is always an AWS responsibility. Distractors: Customer classifies and encrypts its data is a customer responsibility. AWS operates managed-service infrastructure is AWS-managed for managed services. Customer configures IAM users and permissions is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: decide who configures IAM users, roles, and permissions in the account; and decide who configures security groups and network traffic rules around an EC2 instance?
options: Customer configures IAM users and permissions
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
AWS provides physical data center security
Customer classifies and encrypts its data
explanation: Customer configures IAM users and permissions is correct: Identity and access management configuration is a customer responsibility. Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is correct: Configuring network controls such as security groups around customer resources is a customer responsibility. AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is an AWS infrastructure responsibility. AWS provides physical data center security is an AWS responsibility. Customer classifies and encrypts its data is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to decide who classifies and encrypts the customer's own data. Which option best meets the requirement?
options: Customer classifies and encrypts its data
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
explanation: Customer classifies and encrypts its data is correct because Customers own their data and decide how it is classified, encrypted, and who can access it. Distractors: AWS operates managed-service infrastructure is AWS-managed for managed services. Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is a customer responsibility. AWS maintains the global network and Regions is an AWS responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: A design review identifies this requirement: decide who operates the underlying servers and OS of a managed service such as Amazon S3. What should you configure?
options: Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
Customer manages applications on its instances
explanation: AWS operates managed-service infrastructure is correct because For managed services AWS operates the infrastructure, OS, and platform, so customers focus on data and access. Distractors: Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is a customer responsibility. AWS maintains the global network and Regions is an AWS responsibility. Customer manages applications on its instances is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to decide who configures security groups and network traffic rules around an EC2 instance?
options: Customer manages applications on its instances
Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
explanation: Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is correct because Configuring network controls such as security groups around customer resources is a customer responsibility. Distractors: Customer manages applications on its instances is a customer responsibility. Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is a customer task for EC2. AWS maintains the global network and Regions is an AWS responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: decide who maintains the global network backbone, Regions, and Availability Zones; and decide who maintains the host hypervisor and the physical servers.
options: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
Customer manages applications on its instances
explanation: AWS maintains the global network and Regions is correct: AWS maintains the global infrastructure, including Regions, Availability Zones, and the network backbone. AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is correct: AWS is responsible for the infrastructure that runs services, including the hypervisor and physical hardware. Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is a customer responsibility. Customer manages applications on its instances is a customer responsibility. Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is a customer task for EC2.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: An operations team wants to decide who manages the application code and access running on a customer's EC2 instance. Which choice is most appropriate?
options: Customer manages applications on its instances
Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
AWS provides physical data center security
explanation: Customer manages applications on its instances is correct because The customer manages the applications, code, and access running on its own EC2 instances. Distractors: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is a customer task for EC2. AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is an AWS infrastructure responsibility. AWS provides physical data center security is an AWS responsibility.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: During implementation, you must give a specific person long-term credentials to sign in to the account. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: IAM user is correct because An IAM user is an identity with long-term credentials for a person or workload that signs in to the account. Distractors: IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds a second authentication factor.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: An organization has the following requirement: let an application on EC2 obtain temporary credentials to call AWS without stored keys. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: IAM role is correct because An IAM role grants temporary credentials that services or users assume, avoiding long-term keys in code. Distractors: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds a second authentication factor. Root user protection secures the account root user. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: What should be configured first when the goal is to attach the same permissions to many users at once by collecting them together?
explanation: IAM group is correct because An IAM group is a collection of users so you attach a permission set to many identities at once. Distractors: Least privilege grants only the permissions required. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds a second authentication factor. Root user protection secures the account root user.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: Two requirements must be met: require a second authentication factor so a stolen password alone cannot sign in; and centrally manage one workforce sign-in to many AWS accounts and applications. Select the two correct options.
explanation: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is correct: MFA requires an additional factor beyond the password, protecting accounts including the root user. AWS IAM Identity Center is correct: IAM Identity Center centrally manages workforce single sign-on across multiple accounts and applications. IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once. Root user protection secures the account root user.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: Your team needs to follow best practice for the AWS account root user. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Root user protection is correct because Best practice is to secure the root user with MFA, avoid daily use, and not create root access keys. Distractors: Least privilege grants only the permissions required. AWS IAM Identity Center centralizes workforce single sign-on across accounts. IAM policy is a JSON document defining permissions.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: To grant only the permissions a task needs and nothing more, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Least privilege is correct because Least privilege grants only the permissions required for a task, reducing risk if credentials are misused. Distractors: IAM policy is a JSON document defining permissions. Amazon Cognito handles sign-in for an app's external end users. AWS IAM Identity Center centralizes workforce single sign-on across accounts.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: A workload requires that you centrally manage one workforce sign-in to many AWS accounts and applications. What is the correct choice?
explanation: AWS IAM Identity Center is correct because IAM Identity Center centrally manages workforce single sign-on across multiple accounts and applications. Distractors: IAM user is a long-term identity for a person or workload. IAM policy is a JSON document defining permissions. Amazon Cognito handles sign-in for an app's external end users.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: define permissions in a JSON document attached to a user, group, or role; and let an application on EC2 obtain temporary credentials to call AWS without stored keys.
explanation: IAM policy is correct: An IAM policy is a JSON document that defines permissions and is attached to identities or resources. IAM role is correct: An IAM role grants temporary credentials that services or users assume, avoiding long-term keys in code. AWS IAM Identity Center centralizes workforce single sign-on across accounts. Amazon Cognito handles sign-in for an app's external end users. IAM user is a long-term identity for a person or workload.

skill: Define AWS access management capabilities
question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to add sign-up and sign-in for the end users of a customer-facing web or mobile app. What do you select?
explanation: Amazon Cognito is correct because Amazon Cognito provides authentication and user management for an application's external end users. Distractors: IAM user is a long-term identity for a person or workload. IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which AWS feature lets you continuously monitor accounts and workloads for malicious activity using threat intelligence?
explanation: Amazon GuardDuty is correct because GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious or unauthorized activity. Distractors: Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3. AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which option should you implement to automatically scan EC2 instances and container images for software vulnerabilities?
explanation: Amazon Inspector is correct because Amazon Inspector continuously scans workloads such as EC2 and container images for software vulnerabilities. Distractors: AWS WAF filters web requests. Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: You need a solution to discover and protect sensitive data such as personal information stored in Amazon S3. Which option fits best?
explanation: Amazon Macie is correct because Macie uses machine learning to discover and classify sensitive data, such as PII, stored in Amazon S3. Distractors: Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: see a central, prioritized view of security findings across many AWS accounts; and download AWS compliance reports such as SOC and PCI on demand.
explanation: AWS Security Hub is correct: Security Hub aggregates and prioritizes security findings from multiple services into a single view. AWS Artifact is correct: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports and agreements. AWS Firewall Manager centrally manages firewall rules across accounts. Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to filter malicious web requests such as SQL injection before they reach an application. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: AWS WAF is correct because AWS WAF is a web application firewall that filters HTTP and HTTPS requests using rules you define. Distractors: AWS Artifact provides compliance reports on demand. AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit. AWS Shield protects against DDoS attacks.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: A design review identifies this requirement: defend an application against distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks. What should you configure?
explanation: AWS Shield is correct because AWS Shield provides managed DDoS protection, with Standard included and Advanced for enhanced protection. Distractors: AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements. AWS Artifact provides compliance reports on demand. AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to download AWS compliance reports such as SOC and PCI on demand?
explanation: AWS Artifact is correct because AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports and agreements. Distractors: AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit. AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: record account activity and API calls so they can be audited later; and centrally manage firewall rules such as AWS WAF across many accounts in an organization?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail is correct: CloudTrail records account activity and API calls, providing an audit trail for governance and compliance. AWS Firewall Manager is correct: AWS Firewall Manager centrally configures and manages firewall rules across accounts in AWS Organizations. AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings. AWS WAF filters web requests. AWS Shield protects against DDoS attacks.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: An operations team wants to get recommendations that check an account against security and other best practices. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor is correct because Trusted Advisor inspects an account and recommends improvements across several categories including security. Distractors: AWS Firewall Manager centrally manages firewall rules across accounts. Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: During implementation, you must issue and manage the TLS certificates used to secure websites and applications. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) is correct because ACM provisions and manages public and private TLS certificates for use with AWS services. Distractors: Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. AWS Firewall Manager centrally manages firewall rules across accounts. Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: An organization has the following requirement: centrally manage firewall rules such as AWS WAF across many accounts in an organization. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: AWS Firewall Manager is correct because AWS Firewall Manager centrally configures and manages firewall rules across accounts in AWS Organizations. Distractors: Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: What should be configured first when the goal is to create and control the cryptographic keys used to encrypt data across AWS services?

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection

Encryption in transit (TLS) is the general concept of protecting data on the network.

skill: Compare AWS pricing models
question: Your team needs to pay for EC2 by the second with no commitment and no risk that AWS reclaims the capacity. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: On-Demand pricing is correct because On-Demand bills per second with no commitment and is not interrupted, suiting short-term or unpredictable workloads. Distractors: Spot Instances are cheapest but can be interrupted. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend.

skill: Compare AWS pricing models
question: To commit to a specific instance configuration for one or three years to get a large discount, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Reserved Instances is correct because Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration in exchange for a one- or three-year commitment. Distractors: On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend. Spot Instances are cheapest but can be interrupted.

skill: Compare AWS pricing models
question: A workload requires that you commit to a consistent amount of compute spend per hour for one or three years for a flexible discount. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Savings Plans is correct because Savings Plans give lower prices for committing to a consistent dollars-per-hour amount of usage for one or three years. Distractors: On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Spot Instances are cheapest but can be interrupted.

skill: Compare AWS pricing models
question: Two requirements must be met: use spare EC2 capacity at a steep discount for workloads that tolerate interruption; and pay for EC2 by the second with no commitment and no risk that AWS reclaims the capacity. Select the two correct options.
explanation: Spot Instances is correct: Spot Instances use spare capacity at a large discount but can be interrupted, suiting fault-tolerant workloads. On-Demand pricing is correct: On-Demand bills per second with no commitment and is not interrupted, suiting short-term or unpredictable workloads. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend. Volume-based (tiered) pricing lowers per-unit price as usage grows.

skill: Compare AWS pricing models
question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to pay a lower per-unit price as monthly usage of a service such as S3 storage grows. What do you select?

On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price.

skill: Compare AWS pricing models

Distractors: On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend.
